get_tokenizer.summary: Get the tokenizer and set pad token to eos token if it is not already set.
get_tokenizer.description:
Get the tokenizer and set pad token to eos token if it is not already set.

This function initializes a tokenizer from the Hugging Face transformers library
and configures it with appropriate chat templates and padding tokens.

params.name: tokenizer_config
params.description:
A dictionary containing tokenizer configuration.
Required keys:
    - name: The name or path of the pretrained tokenizer
Optional keys:
    - chat_template: The chat template to use.

params.name: get_processor
params.description: Whether to return a processor (via AutoProcessor) instead of a tokenizer.

validate_tensor_consistency.summary: Validate that all tensors have consistent dtypes and devices.
validate_tensor_consistency.description: Validate that all tensors have consistent dtypes and devices.

raises.description: If tensors have different dtypes or devices

eval_collate_fn.description:
Collate function for evaluation.

Takes a list of data samples and combines them into a single batched dictionary
for model evaluation.

Examples:
```{doctest}
>>> import torch
>>> from nemo_rl.data.collate_fn import eval_collate_fn
>>> output = eval_collate_fn(data_batch)
>>> output['message_log'][0]
[{'role': 'user', 'content': 'Hello'}]
```

params.name: data_batch
params.description: List of data samples with message_log, extra_env_info, and idx fields.

returns.description: BatchedDataDict with message_log, extra_env_info, and idx fields.

clipped_pg_loss.summary: Generalized Clipped Policy Gradient loss function w/ KL regularization.
clipped_pg_loss.description:
Generalized Clipped Policy Gradient loss function w/ KL regularization.

Formula:
L(θ) = E_t [ min(r_t(θ) * A_t, clip(r_t(θ), 1-ε, 1+ε) * A_t) ] - β * KL(π_θ || π_ref)

Also supports "Dual-Clipping" from https://arxiv.org/pdf/1912.09729, which
imposes an additional upper bound: $rA << 0$ -> $cA$(clipped)
